zh.lifeSummary: 當Wilhelm IV. von Jülich於1215年出生在海因里希_海普洛文次時，他的父親Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach29歲，母親Mathilde van Limburg24歲。他於1237年3月12日與Margarethe von Geldern結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1297年在海因里希_海普洛文次爾利被列為graf von jülich 1297。他去世於1278年3月16日在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇阿陳納 萊奇亞琛享年63歲，埋葬在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Wilhelm IV. von Jülich于1215年出生在海因里希_海普洛文次时，他的父亲Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach29岁，母亲Mathilde van Limburg24岁。他于1237年3月12日与Margarethe von Geldern结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1297年在海因里希_海普洛文次爾利被列为graf von jülich 1297。他去世于1278年3月16日在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇阿陳納 萊奇亞琛享年63岁，埋葬在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Jülich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto.

variants.name: Juhl
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Arno, Erwin, Heinz, Kurt.

Danish and Norwegian: nickname for someone who was born on Christmas Day or had some other connection with this time of year, from Old Norse jól. This was originally the name of a pagan midwinter festival, which was later appropriated by the Christian Church for celebration of the birth of Christ.
German (of Slavic origin): nickname or topographic name from a Germanized Slavic stem gol ‘naked, barren’.

nameMeanings.name: Wilhelm
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from wil ‘will’ + helm ‘helmet’; an ancient royal name. See also William .

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: IV.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Wilhelm IV. von Jülich was born in 1215, in Rhineland, Prussia, his father, Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach, was 29 and his mother, Mathilde van Limburg, was 24. He married Margarethe von Geldern on 12 March 1237. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. In 1297, his occupation is listed as graf von jülich 1297 in Jülich, Rhineland, Prussia. He died on 16 March 1278, in Aachen, Aachener Reich, Holy Roman Empire, at the age of 63, and was buried in Holy Roman Empire.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Wilhelm IV. von Jülich est né en 1215, à Rhénanie, Prusse, son père, Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Mathilde van Limburg, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Margarethe von Geldern le 12 mars 1237. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. En 1297, sa profession de son indiquée est graf von jülich 1297 à Jülich, Rhénanie, Prusse. Il est décédé le 16 mars 1278, à Aachen, Aachener Reich, Saint-Empire romain, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Saint-Empire romain.

de.lifeSummary: Als Wilhelm IV. von Jülich 1215 in Rheinprovinz, Preußen geboren wurde, war sein Vater Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach 29 und seine Mutter Mathilde van Limburg 24. Er hat am 12. März 1237 Margarethe von Geldern geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1297 ist sein Beruf als graf von jülich 1297 in Jülich, Rheinprovinz, Preußen aufgeführt. Er starb am 16. März 1278 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Aachen, Aachener Reich, Heiliges Römisches Reich und wurde in Heiliges Römisches Reich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Wilhelm IV. von Jülich è nato il nel 1215 nel Rheinprovinz, Preußen, il suo padre, Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Mathilde van Limburg, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Margarethe von Geldern il 12 marzo 1237. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Nel 1297, la sua occupazione è indicata come graf von jülich 1297 nel Jülich, Rheinprovinz, Preußen. Morì il 16 marzo 1278, nel Aachen, Aachener Reich, Heiliges Römisches Reich, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Heiliges Römisches Reich.

ko.lifeSummary: Wilhelm IV. von Jülich(이)가 1215년 프레우ss 리인프로빈츠에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Mathilde van Limburg(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1237년 3월 12일 Margarethe von Geldern(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1297년, 그의 직업은 프레우ss 리인프로빈츠 ju리치에서 graf von jülich 1297이었다. 그는 1278년 3월 16일 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 아악헨어 라이치 아악헨에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Wilhelm IV. von Jülich nasceu em 1215, em Rheinprovinz, Preußen, seu pai, Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Mathilde van Limburg, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Margarethe von Geldern em 12 de março de 1237. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Em 1297, sua profissão aparece como graf von jülich 1297 em Jülich, Rheinprovinz, Preußen. ru.Aachen, Aachener Reich, Holy Roman Empir: аачэн, аачэнир раич, хилигесь рoмищесь раич
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Wilhelm IV. von Jülich родился в 1215 г. в местности раинпровинз, Пруссия, его отцу по имени Wilhelm III. von Jülich-Hengebach было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Mathilde van Limburg, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Margarethe von Geldern 12 марта 1237 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. В 1297 году он работал по профессии graf von jülich 1297 в местности джuлич, раинпровинз, Пруссия. Он умер 16 марта 1278 г. в местности аачэн, аачэнир раич, хилигесь рoмищесь раич в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности хилигесь рoмищесь раич.

Se casó con Margarethe von Geldern el 12 de marzo de 1237. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. En 1297, su ocupación registrada era graf von jülich 1297 en Jülich, Rheinprovinz, Preußen. Murió el 16 de marzo de 1278, en Aachen, Aachener Reich, Heiliges Römisches Reich, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Heiliges Römisches Reich.
